subtitle: Football neighbourhoods, club identity and a demonstration matchday
intro: A five-day football-cultural route that connects La Boca, Núñez, Parque Patricios and central Buenos Aires while keeping one demonstration match as the anchor rather than the entire trip.
routeOverview: The route groups places by football geography. La Boca is explored before the demonstration match so the traveller understands the club and neighbourhood context. River and the northern city form a separate day, while Parque Patricios adds a different club identity without forcing a cross-city rush.

accommodation.area: San Telmo or Microcentro demonstration base
accommodation.note: Final accommodation advice should reflect the traveller's budget, evening plans and preferred transport.

match.competition: Demonstration fixture — not a real booking
match.ticketStatus: Sample only — no valid ticket included
match.idRequirement: Confirm the official requirement attached to the valid ticket
match.bagRule: Travel light and confirm current prohibited-item guidance

timeline.title: Official recheck
timeline.description: Confirm kickoff, ticket instructions, entrance, weather and transport using official sources.

timeline.title: Slow start and early lunch
timeline.description: Keep the morning unhurried and eat before entering the busiest matchday period.

timeline.title: Travel toward La Boca
timeline.description: Use the reviewed route and avoid relying on a single live-navigation option.

timeline.title: Neighbourhood and club context
timeline.description: Use the approved route; do not treat every street as suitable for unguided wandering.

timeline.title: Move toward the stadium perimeter
timeline.description: Stop adding activities and shift attention to ticket, entrance and crowd flow.

timeline.title: Recommended arrival
timeline.description: Allow time for security, ticket problems and finding the correct approach.

timeline.title: Kickoff
timeline.description: Follow official instructions and the conditions attached to the valid ticket.

timeline.title: Post-match exit
timeline.description: Use the reviewed transport plan and expect crowd-related delays.

checklist.label: Valid ticket downloaded and available offline
Stand, entrance and access route confirmed
Phone charged and portable battery packed
Payment plan and small emergency reserve ready

transport.primary: Use the final reviewed route from the accommodation area to the approved approach point, allowing for matchday road closures and crowd controls.
transport.backup: Keep an alternative combination of public transport and a verified pickup point outside the immediate stadium crowd.
transport.postMatch: Do not assume the arrival route will work in reverse. Use the post-match pickup or transport point selected during human review.

supporterAdvice: Approach the experience as a guest rather than trying to perform local supporter identity.
Do not display rival colours or provoke supporters.
Keep valuables unobtrusive and avoid filming people at close range without permission.
Follow supporter segregation, police instructions and the conditions attached to the ticket.
Use Caneta's reviewed visitor route rather than improvising around the stadium.
match.safetyNote: This demonstration does not supply a real fixture, ticket or current crowd plan. A customer version must be checked again after any kickoff change and within 24 hours of the match.
match.verification: Demonstration content created for the prototype — all operational details require publication review

preparationChecklist.label: Confirm travel insurance and entry requirements
Confirm the ticket source, validity and transfer conditions
Recheck the official fixture date and kickoff
Download offline map areas and save backup addresses
Open every companion section once while online
Export a backup of saved companion data before departure

days.summary: Settle in, understand the city layout and introduce the football story without overfilling the first day.
days.routeLogic: Stay around the accommodation and central historic area after arrival. The opening football context is deliberately light and does not require a long cross-city journey.

activities.title: Arrival and practical orientation
activities.description: Check in, confirm transport cards or payment options, save the accommodation address and review the trip's football geography.
activities.practicalNote: Keep the first afternoon light after travel.

activities.title: San Telmo evening and football introduction
activities.description: Take a compact walk through the historic area and use the guide to understand how football clubs map onto neighbourhood identity.
activities.practicalNote: Choose a well-lit return route and keep valuables discreet.
activities.alternative: Use a short café-based football history briefing if arrival is delayed.

days.summary: Understand Boca's stadium, museum and neighbourhood context before the matchday crowds change the area.
days.routeLogic: Travel to the reviewed arrival point, complete one connected La Boca route and leave before the late-evening period. Do not add unrelated cross-city stops.

activities.title: La Bombonera exterior and club context
activities.description: Use the approved approach to understand the stadium's relationship with the neighbourhood and prepare for the following day's match logistics.
activities.practicalNote: Do not infer matchday access from a non-matchday visit.

activities.title: Boca club museum
activities.description: Use the museum visit to connect trophies and personalities to the social history of the club.
activities.practicalNote: Confirm official opening and whether stadium access is included.
activities.alternative: Replace with a longer guided neighbourhood route if the museum is closed.

activities.title: Caminito with neighbourhood context
activities.description: Visit the best-known streets without confusing the tourist zone with the whole neighbourhood. Connect migration, colour and club mythology.
activities.practicalNote: Stay on the reviewed route and follow current local advice.

days.summary: Keep the match as the anchor and reduce optional decisions as the stadium period approaches.
days.routeLogic: The morning stays near the accommodation. Travel toward La Boca early, use only the approved pre-match route and follow the dedicated Matchday tab from mid-afternoon.

activities.title: Fixture and ticket recheck
activities.description: Reconfirm kickoff, ticket instructions, entrance, bag rules, weather and transport.
activities.practicalNote: Use official sources and the final human-reviewed Caneta notes.

activities.title: Early lunch near the base
activities.description: Eat before travelling into the busiest part of the day. Keep alcohol and timing compatible with the valid ticket conditions and personal safety.
activities.practicalNote: Use a verified venue that does not complicate the stadium route.

activities.title: Begin the reviewed matchday route
activities.description: Switch to the dedicated Matchday plan and stop adding optional attractions.
activities.practicalNote: The prototype does not include a valid ticket or real fixture.

days.summary: Use River's northern geography to understand how a different major club relates to the city.
days.routeLogic: Move north once, combine the stadium area with nearby non-football space and return through Palermo rather than crossing back and forth.

activities.title: Estadio Monumental and River context
activities.description: Compare the scale, setting and identity of River's stadium with the previous day's experience.
activities.address: Av. Pres. Figueroa Alcorta 7597, Buenos Aires
activities.practicalNote: Confirm tour or museum availability through official channels.
activities.alternative: Use an exterior architecture and neighbourhood route if no official visit is available.

activities.title: River museum or stadium tour
activities.description: Choose one official visit if available; avoid stacking multiple paid stadium products.
activities.address: Av. Pres. Figueroa Alcorta 7597, Buenos Aires
activities.practicalNote: Book only through an approved source.

activities.title: Palermo recovery afternoon
activities.description: Balance the football-heavy weekend with food, green space or an independent cultural stop.
activities.practicalNote: Choose according to post-match energy rather than forcing another checklist.

days.summary: End with a different club landscape while staying realistic about luggage and departure time.
days.routeLogic: Use one focused Parque Patricios route only if the departure window allows it. Keep the final stop close enough to return for luggage.

activities.title: Huracán and Parque Patricios context
activities.description: Use the exterior stadium and neighbourhood route to understand a club identity beyond Boca and River.
activities.practicalNote: Skip this if it creates pressure around luggage or departure.
activities.alternative: Return to one saved central place instead.
activities.verification: Route and departure logic require review

places.name: Caneta central orientation point
places.footballSignificance: The base for understanding how the city's football neighbourhoods connect.
places.description: A demonstration starting point linking accommodation, transport and the wider trip route.
places.practicalNote: The final location depends on the customer's accommodation.

places.name: San Telmo football orientation
places.footballSignificance: A central base from which the city's club geography becomes easier to understand.
places.description: Historic streets, food and an introduction to neighbourhood identity beyond a stadium checklist.

places.name: La Bombonera
places.footballSignificance: Boca Juniors' stadium and the central anchor of the demonstration match trip.
places.description: Visit first for context, then return only through the final reviewed matchday route.
places.practicalNote: Non-matchday access does not prove matchday access.

places.name: Museo de la Pasión Boquense
places.footballSignificance: A club-focused route through Boca's trophies, personalities and mythology.
places.description: Use it to support the wider social and neighbourhood story, not as a substitute for it.
places.practicalNote: Confirm official opening, admission and included access.

places.name: Caminito
places.footballSignificance: Part of the colour and migration story frequently folded into Boca's global image.
places.description: A tourist-facing route that needs context so it is not mistaken for the whole neighbourhood.
places.practicalNote: Stay on the reviewed route and follow current local advice.

places.name: Verified pre-match lunch
places.footballSignificance: A practical stop that protects the matchday timeline from hunger, queues and improvisation.
places.description: The final customer trip should name a currently verified venue compatible with the stadium route.

places.name: Estadio Monumental
places.footballSignificance: River Plate's stadium creates a strong contrast in scale, setting and club geography.
places.description: Use the area to compare club identity rather than simply collecting stadium photographs.
places.practicalNote: Confirm current access and event restrictions.

places.name: River museum or official tour
places.footballSignificance: An official interpretation of River's history and stadium.
places.description: Select only if the current official product fits the route and customer budget.
places.practicalNote: Use an official or approved booking source.

places.name: Palermo recovery afternoon
places.footballSignificance: A deliberate break that stops a football trip becoming an exhausting stadium checklist.
places.description: Food, parks or independent culture selected according to the traveller's interests.

places.name: Estadio Tomás Adolfo Ducó
places.footballSignificance: Huracán offers a neighbourhood-club perspective beyond the two internationally dominant clubs.
places.description: An exterior and district route for understanding a different strand of Buenos Aires football culture.
places.practicalNote: Confirm route suitability and do not risk the departure window.

places.name: Approved football shirt shop
places.footballSignificance: A place to buy a legitimate local football memory without relying on an unverified seller.
places.description: The final trip should name an official or carefully reviewed independent shop.

places.name: Rainy-day football alternative
places.footballSignificance: Protects the football theme when outdoor routes become unsuitable.
places.description: A reviewed museum, archive, bookshop or café-based football-history activity.

map.title: Buenos Aires football geography
map.summary: The route separates La Boca, Núñez and Parque Patricios into realistic neighbourhood days, with a central base and one dedicated matchday.

guideSections.title: Reading the city through its clubs
guideSections.intro: Caneta's value is the relationship between football and place, not a list of stadium pins.

items.label: Neighbourhood identity
items.text: Treat club identity as connected to local history, migration, class, memory and changing urban geography.

items.label: Beyond Boca and River
items.text: Use at least one additional club landscape to avoid reducing the city to its two most internationally recognised teams.

items.label: Guest behaviour
items.text: Observe and learn without pretending to possess a local supporter identity.

items.label: ¿Por dónde se entra a esta tribuna?
items.text: Where do I enter for this stand?

items.label: ¿A qué hora abren las puertas?
items.text: What time do the gates open?

items.label: ¿Puede marcarlo en el mapa?
items.text: Can you mark it on the map?

guideSections.title: Treat access as operational information
items: A recommendation is not a ticket and a screenshot is not proof that a ticket is valid.
Recheck the official fixture and the precise conditions attached to the customer's ticket.
Do not promise access, transfers, names, entrances or ID rules without current verification.

guideSections.title: Meaning first, booking second
items: Explain why a place or product belongs in the trip before presenting a booking link.
Do not let commission override safety, suitability or route logic.

practicalInfo.title: Transport
items: Store the accommodation address and key route points offline.
Matchday closures and crowd controls can invalidate an ordinary route.
Keep a backup pickup point outside the immediate stadium area.

practicalInfo.title: Money and connectivity
items: Confirm current payment and cash advice during human review.
Do not depend on one phone, one payment method or one live map.
The budget tool stores its entries only in the current browser unless exported.

practicalInfo.title: Verification standard
items: Fixture, ticket, stadium access, transport and safety information must be rechecked closest to the match.
Opening times, prices and tours should come from official or approved sources.
Every customer-facing record should carry a review or verification note.

emergencyInfo.label: Immediate danger
emergencyInfo.text: Use the locally confirmed emergency number and follow official instructions.

emergencyInfo.label: Ticket problem
emergencyInfo.text: Contact the official ticket provider or approved seller; Caneta cannot create or validate access.

emergencyInfo.label: Lost route
emergencyInfo.text: Return to a reviewed public location or accommodation rather than improvising near a stadium crowd.
